title: Week 7 Discussion Post — Privacy Models
html:
cs ethics discussion synthesizing two articles on consumer-data business models. Yahir lands pro-privacy: critiques pay for privacy and personal data economy models for risking a privacy divide and discriminatory targeting; flags re-identification risk on anonymized data.

TL;DR

  • pay for privacy (PFP) models risk widening a privacy divide between rich and poor users.
  • personal data economy (PDE) models claim consumer empowerment but still let companies analyze the data.
  • Even anonymized data can be re-identified — notice-and-choice alone is insufficient consumer protection.
  • Yahir's verdict: comprehensive privacy regulation beats voluntary corporate models — early signal of his pro-regulation lean on data ethics.

cs ethics · pay for privacy · personal data economy · privacy regulation · data ethics
